summary: This repository implements a simplified version of the GPT-2 language model, demonstrating a pattern of building a transformer-based language model from scratch. It provides a basic understanding of how to structure and train such models.

concepts.name: GPT Model
concepts.subtitle: Defines the overall architecture of the language model
concepts.description: The GPT class represents the core of the language model, encapsulating the transformer architecture, embedding layers, and the final linear layer for output. It exists to provide a structured way to organize the components of the model, making it easier to understand and modify. The key insight here is how the different components are tied together to form a cohesive model.
concepts.ask: How does the GPT model integrate various components like embeddings, transformer blocks, and the final linear layer?

concepts.name: Transformer Block
concepts.subtitle: A fundamental building block of the transformer architecture
concepts.description: The Block class represents a single transformer block, which is a crucial component of the GPT model. It exists to handle the self-attention mechanism and the feed-forward network (FFN) within the transformer architecture. The key insight is how self-attention allows the model to weigh the importance of different input elements relative to each other.
concepts.ask: What role does the transformer block play in the overall architecture of the GPT model?

concepts.name: Causal Self-Attention
concepts.subtitle: Enables the model to attend to previous tokens in the sequence
concepts.description: The CausalSelfAttention class implements the causal self-attention mechanism, which is essential for the transformer blocks. It exists to allow the model to focus on relevant parts of the input sequence when generating text. The key insight here is how causal self-attention prevents the model from attending to future tokens, which is crucial for tasks like text generation.
concepts.ask: How does causal self-attention contribute to the model's ability to generate coherent text?

concepts.name: Embedding Layers
concepts.subtitle: Maps input tokens to vectors in a high-dimensional space
concepts.description: The embedding layers are responsible for converting input tokens into vectors that the model can process. They exist to provide a way to represent discrete tokens in a continuous space, enabling the model to capture nuanced relationships between tokens. The key insight is how these embeddings are learned during training to effectively represent the input data.
concepts.ask: What is the purpose of the embedding layers in the GPT model, and how do they contribute to its performance?

concepts.name: Training Loop
concepts.subtitle: Defines how the model is trained on the dataset
concepts.description: The training loop is responsible for iterating over the dataset, computing losses, and updating the model parameters. It exists to provide a structured way to optimize the model's performance on the given task. The key insight here is how the training loop integrates with the model architecture to achieve the desired outcomes.
concepts.ask: How does the training loop interact with the GPT model to optimize its performance during training?

concepts.name: Data Pipeline
concepts.subtitle: Handles the preparation and loading of the dataset
concepts.description: The data pipeline is responsible for preparing and loading the dataset used for training and evaluation. It exists to provide a clean and efficient way to interact with the data, ensuring that the model is trained on high-quality inputs. The key insight is how the data pipeline supports the overall training process by providing the necessary data in the right format.
concepts.ask: What role does the data pipeline play in supporting the training and evaluation of the GPT model?

concepts.name: Model Configuration
concepts.subtitle: Defines the hyperparameters and settings for the model
concepts.description: The GPTConfig class represents the configuration of the model, including hyperparameters and settings. It exists to provide a centralized way to manage the model's configuration, making it easier to experiment with different settings and reproduce results. The key insight here is how the configuration affects the model's behavior and performance.
concepts.ask: How does the model configuration influence the behavior and performance of the GPT model?

concepts.name: Loss Estimation
concepts.subtitle: Estimates the loss of the model on the validation set
concepts.description: The estimate_loss function is used to estimate the loss of the model on the validation set. It exists to provide a way to evaluate the model's performance during training, helping to identify overfitting or underfitting. The key insight is how loss estimation supports the training process by providing feedback on the model's performance.
concepts.ask: What is the purpose of loss estimation in the context of training the GPT model?
